_schema.name: token_estimate_library
_schema.purpose: Per-use-case token consumption estimates for the Investment-Case Simulator and Hepburn diagnostic. Every band is anchored to named public evidence, provenance-graded, dated, and machine-validated (validate_token_estimate_library.py). Methodology: HA_EnterpriseAI_TokenEstimateLibrary_Methodology_v1_2026-07-04.md (Enterprise AI Adoption Project folder).
_schema.units: tokens per transaction unless stated; USD per 1,000,000 tokens for prices (see benchmark_price_sheet.json)

band_semantics.low: well-engineered deployment (lean context, tuned retrieval, disciplined outputs)
band_semantics.high: naive deployment (context stuffing, no output discipline, default settings)
band_semantics.note: Bands are behavioural, NOT statistical percentiles. Width is honest uncertainty plus real deployment spread. Consumption optimisation moves you within the band; it must NOT be stacked on top of the low band.

_schema.provenance_scale: TAIL standard: 1A filed/audited · 1B first-party docs/blog/telemetry · 2A analyst citing first-party · 2B journalist/single-source · 3A podcast/conference paraphrase · 3B community-measured (leaderboards, traced runs) · 3C editorial
_schema.anchor_types: A first-party telemetry · B dollar-per-task back-derivation · C community-measured logs · D first-principles composition from conversion constants · E practitioner consensus · F editorial
_schema.reference_model_rule: Every band is stated for the named default_model and reasoning_mode. Changing model applies model_adjustment_factors — consumption is NOT model-invariant.
_schema.evidence_sweep: 2026-07-04, three-stream research fan-out (per-use-case anchors; optimisation levers; model-change effects). ~45 sources fetched/verified.

_changelog.change: Initial library. Coverage 6 -> 15 use cases. v0.1 bands retained where evidence supported them (code_assistant chat, enterprise_rag_search, document_claims_processing, back_office_reconciliation, contact_centre_summarisation). Material restatement: marketing_content transaction redefined to iterated asset (13-round median, Anthropic Economic Index, 1B) — v0.1 single-shot band preserved as variant. Volume guidance: code-assistant 60 interactions/dev/day reclassified from mid to high; new band 10/30/80 (3C, flagged). 11 of 15 use cases now carry non-editorial load-bearing anchors.

tokens_per_page_text.src: AWS Bedrock pricing worked example (500); general range; structured docs (invoices) run ~2,500 incl. layout

tokens_per_hour_audio_native.src: Microsoft TechCommunity — ~1,000 audio tokens/min (GPT-4o-class audio tokenisation). 5x the transcript route — architecture choice matters.

tokens_per_1000_lines_code.src: consistent across community calculators; ~10 tokens/line, code 1.5-2x prose density

vision_tokens_per_512px_tile.src: OpenAI vision docs (hi-detail); typical invoice image ~900 input tokens

language_fertility_multiplier_vs_english.src: arXiv 2605.24718 (25 European languages, May 2026) + community measurements; values normalised to English = 1.2 tokens/word

code_assistant.transaction_def: one chat/edit interaction in-IDE (context-heavy). Autocomplete is a separate variant — do not blend.
code_assistant.confidence: Band unchanged from v0.1 — no direct public telemetry for IDE chat interactions exists. Consistent with practitioner consensus and with SKU price ceilings once caching is assumed (see derivation), but still editorial.

anchors.value: repo-context chat interactions cluster 3-12K input in practitioner guidance
Copilot-class SKU $19-39/seat/mo (incl. ~60% packaging margin per TAIL marginPcts) bounds underlying AI spend at ~$8-16/seat/mo

code_assistant.derivation: v0.1 band retained. Consistency check: mid band x 30 interactions/day x 21 days at Sonnet list = ~$21/seat/mo raw; with 70% cache-read on input = ~$14/seat/mo — inside the $8-16 SKU-implied AI floor. The reconciliation only works WITH caching, which is evidence vendors run cached — and why naive direct-API replication can cost more than the SKU.

chat_interactions_per_dev_per_day.note: REPLACES the flat 60/dev/day used in the 18 Jun worked example — 60 is now graded high-side, not mid. No robust public source exists; client telemetry required.

autocomplete.note: Single source (onprem.ai role model). GitHub excludes completions from paid credit metering — signal that marginal cost is negligible; runs on small custom models, not frontier.

optimisation_profile.note: Repo/system context is highly cacheable (50-70% hit realistic). Autocomplete already routes to tiny models industry-wide.

code_assistant.path_to_upgrade: Client IDE telemetry (Cursor/Copilot usage exports) via gated diagnostic; any first-party interaction-level disclosure.

agentic_coding.transaction_def: one delegated coding task (a Claude Code / Cursor-agent / Devin-class session-task: multi-turn, multi-tool, context replayed each call). Input counted as physical tokens processed INCLUDING cache reads — see billing_assumptions.

billing_assumptions.src: dirac.run OpenRouter effective-pricing analysis (agent traffic 65-87% cache-read share); Anthropic caching docs

agentic_coding.confidence: Best-anchored agentic use case in the library. Output side is 1B (Anthropic telemetry); input side is back-derived from three independent dollar anchors agreeing within ~2x.

anchors.value: ~4 turns/session, ~10 agent actions/prompt (2% of sessions >100), ~3,200 tokens output/turn => ~13K output/session median; users avg 20 hrs/week active runtime
Anthropic revised average Claude Code cost $6 -> $13/dev/active-day (Apr 2026); $150-250/dev/mo; 90% of users <$30/day
Aider polyglot single-shot tasks: $0.006-0.29/task across models; SWE-bench Verified cap 2M uncached + 20M cached-read tokens/problem (rarely hit); SWE-agent community budget $3/instance
anchors.source: Aider leaderboard; Epoch AI SWE-bench methodology

anchors.value: Cursor docs illustrative heavy agent request: ~350K input + 20K output (~$1.50 at Sonnet rates); Devin: 1 ACU ~ 15 min work at $2.00-2.25 => $8-9/hour of agent work

agentic_coding.derivation: Output band centred on Anthropic's measured ~13K/session. Input band back-derived: $13/day at ~0.65/task (mid band, 80% cache-read, Sonnet rates) implies ~20 tasks/active-day at the observed intensity — coherent with Cursor's 350K illustrative request and Epoch's 2M/20M cap as the high end.

dollar_anchors.label: cost per delegated task
dollar_anchors.includes: LLM only, cache-adjusted (see billing_assumptions); brackets Aider single-shot low end and SWE-agent $3 budget high end

tasks_per_dev_per_active_day.note: high end back-derived from $13/day / $0.65/task; median usage is lower

optimisation_profile.note: Caching IS the economics here: at 80% cache-read share, effective input price ~0.28x list. Cache-hostile prompt design (mutating prefixes) is the costliest single mistake — documented production fix took hit rate 7% -> 84%, total cost -59% (ProjectDiscovery). Context editing cuts 84% of tokens on 100-turn runs. NOTE: the architecture itself runs ~4-15x chat token volumes — these levers contain agentic cost, they do not make agents cheap.

model_sensitivity: agentic turn count (input x turns drives cost; documented 5.5x cost delta with output flat)
tokenizer_generation_change (1.0-1.35x input at flat prices, Claude 4.6->4.7 case)
agentic_coding.path_to_upgrade: Client Claude Code / Cursor admin analytics (per-seat token telemetry is exportable) — fastest 1A upgrade in the library.

contact_centre_summarisation.transaction_def: one inbound contact summarised/drafted (agent-assist: summary + suggested response; QA scoring as fan-out)
contact_centre_summarisation.confidence: v0.1 band retained and now bracketed by an independent operations-wiki cost model. Upgraded 3C -> 3B.

anchors.value: 500-seat centre, 8,000 interactions/day running summarisation + copilot + QA: $2,000-8,000/mo API cost => $0.008-0.033/interaction full-assist stack
anchors.source: WFM Labs wiki — GenAI impact on contact centre operations

anchors.value: LLM QA evaluation $0.05-0.20 per 100%-coverage evaluation (vs $3-8 human)
10-min contact transcript ~ 1,700-2,200 tokens + system prompt => mid 3K input coheres

contact_centre_summarisation.derivation: Mid band x fan-out 2 at Haiku = ~$0.011/interaction — inside the WFM $0.008-0.033 bracket. First-principles transcript composition agrees with mid input.

contacts_per_agent_per_day.note: industry norm, no single citable source; client WFM data replaces this trivially

optimisation_profile.note: System prompt + policy context highly cacheable; QA scoring is batchable (async by nature, 50% off).

model_sensitivity: routing headroom large — task rarely needs frontier
contact_centre_summarisation.path_to_upgrade: Client CCaaS platform token/cost exports; vendor (Nice/Genesys/Five9) disclosures.

customer_support_chatbot.transaction_def: one contained (deflected) customer conversation end-to-end — multi-turn totals INCLUDING per-turn RAG context; not per-turn
customer_support_chatbot.confidence: Well-bracketed by vendor per-resolution prices (ceiling) and cloud worked examples (floor). Turn-count and RAG-depth assumptions drive the band.

anchors.value: Per-resolution vendor prices: Intercom Fin $0.99; Zendesk $1.50-2.00; Gorgias ~$0.60-1.27; Decagon ~$0.50 (reported) — all-in prices incl. margin; ceilings not token measures
Underlying LLM cost $0.01-0.10 per bot conversation
AWS Bedrock worked example: ~200 input (query + retrieved context) + ~100 output per turn; 10K queries/mo = $21.11 on Sonnet-class (~$0.002/turn)
anchors.source: AWS ML blog — Demystifying Bedrock pricing for a chatbot

anchors.value: 3-8 turns per contained conversation; LLM containment 40-60% vs 15-25% decision-tree bots

customer_support_chatbot.derivation: Per-conversation band = AWS-style per-turn shape x 3-8 turns with RAG context per turn. Mid at Sonnet = ~$0.015/conversation — inside the $0.01-0.10 LLM bracket, and ~1.5-6% of the Fin $0.99 resolution price (the rest is vendor platform + margin — Gap #1 quantified for support).

dollar_anchors.label: LLM cost per contained conversation
dollar_anchors.includes: LLM only; vendor per-resolution prices sit 10-60x above this

volume_hints.note: volume = monthly inbound contacts x deflection share (40-60% LLM containment, 3B) — client-known quantities

optimisation_profile.note: The routing showcase: heterogeneous difficulty, FAQ-heavy. Semantic cache realistic hit band 40-60% on FAQ traffic (NOT the 95% of vendor marketing — that is match accuracy). Stacking: semantic-cache hits remove requests; routing applies to misses only.

model_sensitivity: multilingual fertility if serving non-English customers (x1.25-2.6 input)
customer_support_chatbot.path_to_upgrade: Client bot-platform analytics; per-resolution invoice reconciliation against measured conversations.

enterprise_rag_search.transaction_def: one knowledge query answered over retrieved documents
enterprise_rag_search.confidence: v0.1 band retained — practitioner consensus is convergent but NO large-scale first-party telemetry of production RAG token distributions exists publicly. The most consensus-dependent band in the library.

anchors.value: 5-20 chunks retrieved, 2,000-10,000 tokens assembled context per query; keep <8K; chunks 400-512 tokens with 10-20% overlap
anchors.source: PremAI production guide; AI21; Redis — convergent practitioner guidance

anchors.value: Light-RAG bot floor: ~$0.002/query at Sonnet-class on small context (AWS worked example)
Context-rot evidence: 18-model test shows degradation as context grows — engineered-low band (lean context) is also the QUALITY optimum, not just the cost one

enterprise_rag_search.derivation: Band = query + system (~500-1K) + 5-20 chunks x ~450 tokens. The 3:1-5:1 input skew note from v0.1 (multi-model-token-estimates-2026-04-04.md, apac-ai-intel repo) understates heavy retrieval — production skew runs 10-20:1.

dollar_anchors.label: LLM cost per query, cheap-model reference
dollar_anchors.includes: LLM only; embedding/vector-store costs excluded (typically <5% at query time)

queries_per_knowledge_worker_per_day.note: no public telemetry; search-replacement behaviour varies enormously

optimisation_profile.note: Reranking is the rare win-win: ~1/3 the context at matched-or-better answer quality. Caching helps little here (chunks are per-query; only the system prompt caches — 10-30% of input). Offline/batch RAG pipelines reach 70-80%.

model_sensitivity: reasoning_toggle if agentic retrieval loops added (3-20x output + turn growth)
enterprise_rag_search.path_to_upgrade: Client RAG-pipeline logs (context sizes are logged by default in LangSmith-class tooling); any vendor telemetry disclosure.

document_claims_processing.transaction_def: one document/claim processed end-to-end (may fan out to several model calls: extract, validate, decide, draft)
document_claims_processing.confidence: v0.1 band retained; now bracketed by three independent per-document/per-page dollar anchors. Fan-out remains the softest factor.

anchors.value: Direct PDF -> frontier LLM ~$0.13/page (Gemini 2.5 Pro-class); decoupled OCR-then-LLM ~$0.05/document; cheap end Gemini-Flash-class $0.17 per 1,000 pages
anchors.source: Parsli LLM-OCR cost analysis (vendor; self-interested on the decoupled claim)

anchors.value: Typical invoice ~2,500 tokens per extraction; 10-page contract 20K+ tokens
anchors.source: Mindee LLM vs OCR cost comparison

anchors.value: STRUCTURED-OUTPUT INVERSION: a scanned page with 80 records => 12,000+ output tokens of JSON from ~500 tokens of raw text — output can exceed input 20x on dense extraction
500-1,000 input tokens/page text; ~900 tokens/page as vision input (tile maths)

document_claims_processing.derivation: Band = 5-30 page-equivalents x per-page constants, fan-out per pipeline stage. Mid x fan-out mid at Sonnet = ~$0.16/claim — inside the composite $0.05-0.50 bracket.

dollar_anchors.label: cost per document processed end-to-end
dollar_anchors.includes: LLM calls across pipeline (fan_out); OCR preprocessing excluded

volume_hints.note: volume = client's claims/documents per month — a known business quantity; this use case has the LEAST volume uncertainty

optimisation_profile.note: The batch showcase: document pipelines are async by nature — flat 50% off everything. Schema/instruction prefix caches well across documents. Structured-output discipline (minimal JSON schema) contains the inversion risk.

model_sensitivity: structured-output inversion (output up to 20x input on dense extraction)
vision vs text ingestion route (~2x input delta)
document_claims_processing.path_to_upgrade: Client pipeline telemetry; insurer/TPA case studies with disclosed volumetrics.

back_office_reconciliation.transaction_def: one reconciliation run / exception handled (ERP agent: fetch, compare, explain, propose journal)
back_office_reconciliation.confidence: DECLARED VOID — the July 2026 evidence sweep found NO public token or per-task LLM cost data for ERP/reconciliation agents from any vendor (Hypatos, V7, D365 agents, NetSuite/SAP checked). Band is editorial, composed from document-extraction economics + agent fan-out. Treat outputs for this archetype as illustrative-only until client telemetry exists.

anchors.value: Manual baseline $12-15 per hand-processed invoice — an economic ceiling, NOT a token anchor
Band composed from document_claims per-page constants + exception-handling context; no direct evidence

back_office_reconciliation.derivation: Composition only: exception context (ledger lines + policy + matched docs, ~3-15K) + explanation/journal output. Mid x fan-out mid at Sonnet = ~$0.10/exception — plausible against a $12-15 manual ceiling but unverified.

volume_hints.note: volume = client's monthly reconciliation exceptions — known business quantity

optimisation_profile.note: Async by nature — batch applies to almost everything. Policy/schema context caches across exceptions.

back_office_reconciliation.path_to_upgrade: ONLY client telemetry — this is the flagship case for 'we size it from priors, then measure yours in a week'. Watch: Microsoft/SAP agent pricing disclosures (Microsoft testing cheaper models for Copilot agents is the visible cost-pressure signal).

marketing_content.transaction_def: one content asset produced via a REALISTIC ITERATED workflow (median 13 rounds of drafting/refinement — Anthropic EI). RESTATED from v0.1, which priced a single-shot draft; that band survives as variant single_shot_draft.
marketing_content.confidence: The iteration multiplier is the best-evidenced fact here (1B); per-turn sizes are composed. v0.1's single-shot framing understated real per-asset consumption ~10x — a material correction.

anchors.value: Median chat conversation producing a blog/article = 13 user turns; marketing-manager-task conversations ~2.5x tokens of editor-task ones
1,500-word article = ~2,000-2,250 output tokens single-shot (word->token composition agrees with community calculators)

marketing_content.derivation: Iterated band = single-shot shape x 10-15 rounds with history replay on input and partial regeneration on output. Single-shot at Sonnet = ~$0.02/asset (matches the circulating ~$0.03/article figure); iterated at Flash = ~$0.02-0.06/asset.

dollar_anchors.label: LLM cost per finished asset (iterated, cheap-model reference)
dollar_anchors.includes: full iteration history; human time excluded (and dominates true cost)

single_shot_draft.note: v0.1 band preserved. Valid for API/batch generation pipelines (no human iteration).

optimisation_profile.note: Cheapest inference of the set — the floor-vs-SKU illustration survives. Iteration discipline (fewer rounds via better briefs) is a CONSUMPTION lever worth more than any billing lever here; brand/style guides cache well.

model_sensitivity: verbosity_same_class (1.5-2.6x output — directly multiplies cost here)
marketing_content.path_to_upgrade: Team workspace analytics (Claude/ChatGPT team token reporting); content-ops platform telemetry.

meeting_intelligence.transaction_def: one meeting-hour processed via the TRANSCRIPT route (STT -> text -> LLM summary/actions). Audio-native route is a variant at ~5x input.
meeting_intelligence.confidence: Strong: first-party transcript-volume telemetry and first-principles composition agree within 20%. The architecture fork (transcript vs audio-native) is the main sizing risk — 5x input difference.

anchors.value: 90-minute meeting ~ 15,000-20,000 transcript tokens (=> ~10-13K/hour); chunked summarisation at 4K + 500 overlap standard
Audio-native ~1,000 tokens/minute => ~60K/hour — 5x the transcript route
STT anchor $0.006/min = $0.36/hour — STT typically exceeds the LLM summarisation cost

meeting_intelligence.derivation: Input = transcript tokens/hour + prompt; output = summary + actions. Mid x fan-out 1.5 at Haiku = ~$0.024/meeting-hour LLM — small next to the $0.36/hr STT line, which the investment case must carry separately.

meeting_hours_per_employee_per_week.note: meeting-heavy roles; org calendars make this measurable instantly

audio_native.note: per meeting-hour at ~1K audio tokens/min. Choose route deliberately — it is a 5x input decision.

optimisation_profile.note: Fully async — batch everything. Small models handle summarisation well (routing headroom large). Naive = frontier + audio-native + sync; engineered = Haiku-class + transcript + batch.

model_sensitivity: route choice transcript vs audio-native (5x input — bigger than any model swap)
meeting_intelligence.path_to_upgrade: Vendor telemetry (Fireflies/Granola/Zoom disclose little); client pilot measurement is trivial (transcripts are logged).

deep_research.transaction_def: one agentic research report (multi-step: search, fetch, read, synthesise — 40-180 tool steps)

billing_assumptions.src: Gemini Deep Research analyses report 50-70% of input cached

deep_research.confidence: The best-measured agentic use case publicly: a full token trace, vendor pricing, and independent lab measurements all exist and cohere.

anchors.value: Full single-query trace (o4-mini-deep-research): 60,506 input / 22,883 output (20,416 of it reasoning) = $0.30 inference + $0.77 search fees + $0.03 code = $1.10 total; 45 searches, 24 page-opens, 180 steps
10 test queries on o3-deep-research: ~$10/query average, up to ~$30/call; o4-mini-DR ~$0.92/query
Gemini Deep Research: standard ~80 searches, ~250K input (50-70% cached), ~60K output ~ $2/task; Max tier ~160 searches, ~900K input, ~80K output ~ $5/task; search grounding fees add $1-2/task
anchors.source: TokenCost / MindStudio analyses of Google pricing

deep_research.derivation: Band spans the measured o4-mini trace (low) through Gemini standard (mid) to Gemini Max (high). Reasoning share of output ~50-90%. Tool fees (search grounding) can equal or exceed inference — carry separately in the cost model.

dollar_anchors.label: cost per research report (inference only)
dollar_anchors.includes: LLM inference; search/grounding fees EXCLUDED (add $0.8-2.2/task where applicable)

optimisation_profile.note: Provider-side caching already embedded (50-70%). The user-controllable levers are breadth caps (searches/steps), effort dial (1.8-3.5x output span), and tier choice (mini-class does 80% of reports at ~1/10th cost — the o4-mini vs o3 gap is 10x).

model_sensitivity: agentic breadth (searches x pages read drives input 15x across tiers)
tool fees priced per-call not per-token (invisible to token estimates)
deep_research.path_to_upgrade: Own-account usage exports (admin consoles expose per-feature token draw).

translation_localisation.transaction_def: 1,000 words of source text translated (document-grade, with instructions/glossary context)
translation_localisation.confidence: Tightest band in the library — the task determines the token count (source text in, translated text out). The only symmetric use case. Target-language fertility is the main multiplier.

anchors.value: Budget heuristic 1,000 chars ~ 250 in + 250 out; LLM translation ~$0.60 per MILLION source words on nano-class models — ~186x cheaper than Google NMT, ~230x cheaper than DeepL per-character pricing
1,000 words = ~1,333 tokens + instructions/glossary ~200-600; output = same content in target language x fertility
Fertility multipliers vs English: Romance 1.25-1.45x, Germanic 1.4-1.6x, Slavic 1.8-2.1x, CJK 2-4x

translation_localisation.derivation: Composition and the dollar heuristic agree within 15%. At mistral_small the mid band = ~$0.0006/1,000 words — matching the published $0.60/M-words figure almost exactly.

dollar_anchors.label: LLM cost per 1,000 source words
dollar_anchors.includes: LLM only; review/QA workflow excluded; fan_out high covers back-translation QA

volume_hints.note: volume = client's localisation word count — contractually known

optimisation_profile.note: The clearest arbitrage in the library: batchable (50% off), small models suffice (frontier -> small = ~90-95% off), glossaries cache. Naive = frontier + sync; engineered = small + batch. The 186-230x gap vs legacy MT vendor pricing is the sharpest SKU-vs-floor illustration TAIL holds.

model_sensitivity: tokenizer fertility DOMINANT (output-side x1.25-4 by target language)
quality floor varies by language pair — cheap models degrade on low-resource pairs
translation_localisation.path_to_upgrade: Client TMS integration telemetry; per-language measured fertility on client content.

text_to_sql_analytics.transaction_def: one natural-language analytics question answered against a governed schema (NL -> SQL -> execute -> explain)
text_to_sql_analytics.confidence: Widest method variance of any use case — published per-query totals span 700 to 150,000 tokens depending on prompting architecture. The band prices the sane middle; the derivation carries the extremes.

anchors.value: Tokens/query by method: DAIL-SQL ~700; PTD-SQL 1,855; C3 2,803; DIN-SQL 9,126
Agentic orchestration (SQLConductor): 7.1K input + 12.2K OUTPUT per query — reasoning/agentic methods flip the ratio output-heavy; still 2-16x fewer tokens than CHESS/Alpha-SQL-class agents
Production correction: Dataherald cut 150K -> 25.5K tokens/query (-83%) via few-shot pruning + schema pruning under monitoring
~$0.01/query typical on GPT-4o-class; schema prefix caches at 90% off

text_to_sql_analytics.derivation: Low = lean single-call (DAIL-class). Mid = few-shot + schema context. High = agentic/reasoning with self-correction. The Dataherald case is the canonical naive-band story: unmonitored schema stuffing ran 150K/query — 17x our high band — until measured.

optimisation_profile.note: Schema pruning is the measured big win (-83%, production). Schema/few-shot prefix is the textbook cache candidate (90% off, stable across queries). Route simple aggregations to small models.

model_sensitivity: reasoning_toggle FLIPS the io ratio (output 200 -> 12,000 on the same question)
text_to_sql_analytics.path_to_upgrade: Client BI-copilot logs; per-method A/B measurement in a week-long pilot.

email_crm_drafting.transaction_def: one personalised outreach/CRM email drafted, INCLUDING enrichment context in the prompt (research context is the driver, not the email)
email_crm_drafting.confidence: Editorial with consistent vendor-price ceilings. Generation is trivially cheap; enrichment/research context (and any agentic research fan-out) is where real AI-SDR token bills live. No SDR vendor publishes token telemetry.

anchors.value: Drafts ~50-200 input + 250-300 output; recruiter-style 200 in / 300 out
AiSDR <$1 per email ALL-IN (~$900/mo product — margin-dominated ceiling); Sendr ~$0.12 per enriched personalised lead
Email drafts = 7% of work-related Claude conversation artifacts (volume context)

email_crm_drafting.derivation: Raw LLM cost per drafted email ~$0.001-0.01 — negligible against the $0.12-1.00 all-in vendor prices. The investment case for AI-SDR tooling is therefore about workflow and data, not inference cost.

dollar_anchors.label: raw LLM cost per personalised email
dollar_anchors.includes: drafting + enrichment context in prompt; agentic prospect-research fan-out excluded

optimisation_profile.note: Small models suffice; templates/brand voice cache; enrichment batches overnight. Cost is a rounding error either way — optimisation matters only at very high volume.

model_sensitivity: if agentic research per prospect is added, consumption moves to deep_research-lite shape (10-50x)

voice_agents.transaction_def: one minute of live voice conversation — LLM component only (STT/TTS carried separately; TTS is often the largest stack line). History replays each turn (~4 turns/min) so input grows with call length.
voice_agents.confidence: Back-derived from published per-minute dollar figures across two independent calculator/platform sources; no vendor publishes measured tokens/minute. Latency requirements constrain model choice more than cost does.

anchors.value: LLM component: GPT-4o-mini-class ~$0.003/min; Sonnet-class $0.02-0.04/min; full stack $0.05-0.30/min with TTS often largest (ElevenLabs $0.08-0.12/min); STT 3-8%
anchors.source: Softcery calculator; Retell AI pricing analysis

anchors.value: ~150 wpm, ~4 turns/minute, 1.8x 'reality factor' for history replay + function calls + barge-in; input grows quadratically with call length
Audio-native alternative ~1,000 tokens/min input

voice_agents.derivation: Band back-derived so that Sonnet-reference cost/minute lands inside the published $0.02-0.04; mini-class models land near $0.002-0.003/min on the same band — both anchors satisfied within 2x.

volume_hints.note: volume = call minutes/month — telephony metrics the client already has

optimisation_profile.note: History replay makes caching structural (system prompt + growing transcript prefix). Aggressive history truncation/summarisation caps the quadratic. Batch inapplicable (real-time).

model_sensitivity: latency floor constrains model class (small/fast models mandatory — cost follows)
audio-native vs pipeline architecture (different token economics entirely)
voice_agents.path_to_upgrade: Voice-platform (Retell/Vapi-class) per-call token exports; client telephony pilot.

hr_legal_document_review.transaction_def: one document reviewed (contract risk review, policy compliance check, or CV batch-equivalent) — reasoning-heavy read-and-judge
hr_legal_document_review.confidence: The load-bearing dollar anchors are a named exec's podcast disclosures (3A) plus a vendor estimate (3C) — they agree within ~2x of the composed band, but nothing here is measured telemetry. High fan-out end (complex drafting) is very soft.

anchors.value: Harvey (named exec, on record): single complex drafting query ~ $20 compute; contract review across 100K documents ~ $20,000 => ~$0.20/document bulk review; a major client burned its full-year agentic budget in 3-4 months
50-page contract: 20K in + 26K reasoning out; 100-page risk review 50K in + 38.5K out; 2-page resume 1.1K in + ~3.2K reasoning out (output-heavy 1:3)
Harvey ~$1,200/lawyer/mo seat price (reported) — economic ceiling

hr_legal_document_review.derivation: Mid (20K/12K at Sonnet ~ $0.24) sits on the Harvey bulk-review ~$0.20/doc anchor. The $20 drafting query = high band x high fan-out (agentic drafting loops) — carried as fan-out, not inflated into the base band. Reasoning mode is assumed ON: this is the use case where turning it off most misprices.

dollar_anchors.label: cost per document reviewed
dollar_anchors.includes: single review pass to moderate fan-out; complex agentic drafting ($20/query) excluded from this bracket

optimisation_profile.note: Bulk review batches (50% off). Playbook/policy context caches across documents. Effort dial matters here more than anywhere: medium effort ~ -40-50% cost for single-digit accuracy points — a risk-appetite decision the client must own, not a default.

model_sensitivity: generation upgrades shift reasoning-token behaviour unpredictably (documented both directions)
hr_legal_document_review.path_to_upgrade: Legal-ops platform telemetry; client pilot on own contract set (a week of logs beats every anchor above).

_families.consumption: reduce tokens processed — these define WHERE IN THE BAND a deployment sits. The low band already assumes them. NEVER stack consumption savings on top of the low band.
_families.billing: reduce price per token — these apply to whatever consumption lands at, and DO stack (multiplicatively, with the stated exceptions).

prompt_caching.mechanism: provider stores KV-cache of a byte-identical prompt prefix; repeat requests bill cached tokens at reduced rate
prompt_caching.applies_to: cached share of INPUT tokens only

value.providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google (Gemini 3.x/2.5), AWS Bedrock — all 0.1x as of 2026-07-04
value.write_premiums: Anthropic 1.25x (5-min) / 2x (1-hr); OpenAI free+automatic; Gemini explicit-cache storage fee $1.00-4.50/M tokens/hr; Bedrock +25% on Claude, none on Nova

realistic_hit_rates.src: dirac.run OpenRouter telemetry; Helicone/Vellum production bands; ProjectDiscovery case (7%->84% hit, -59% total cost)

prompt_caching.conditions: byte-identical prefix >= provider minimum (512-4,096 tokens); requests within TTL; stable tool/system preamble — any upstream change invalidates downstream

batch_flex.mechanism: async submission on spare capacity (<=24h; most jobs 1-6h); or synchronous flex tiers at same discount with availability risk

value.providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google — flat 50% as of 2026-07-04; inverse lever exists (priority tiers cost 1.8-2.5x)

batch_flex.stacking: multiplies with caching (vendor-confirmed; exception: Gemini 3.1 Pro batch cache-read is 0.2x of batch input, not 0.1x)

model_routing.mechanism: classifier/cascade sends easy queries to cheap models, hard ones to frontier
model_routing.applies_to: entire routed request (input + output at the cheaper model's price)

value.measured: 45-85% cost reduction at ~95% frontier quality (RouteLLM: >85% MT-Bench, 45% MMLU, 35% GSM8K; best router needed frontier on only 14% of calls); FrugalGPT up to 98% on 2023-era models
value.vendor_claims: Martian 92% and NotDiamond claims are unverified/mixed — RouterArena ranks NotDiamond #12 for over-selecting expensive models

model_routing.conditions: heterogeneous difficulty mix; calibration data; ongoing evals
model_routing.quality_tradeoff: definitional — 'matched quality' is benchmark-specific; the residual % IS the trade
model_routing.stacking: changes the base price; other levers' percentages then apply. Routing and distillation are the SAME lever — never count both on the same traffic

fine_tune_distill.mechanism: replace frontier model with a small model fine-tuned on the narrow task

value.per_token_gap: ~85% (current small-vs-frontier list gap) plus shorter prompts (fewer few-shot examples)
value.caveat: OpenAI fine-tuning platform winding down (July 2026, closed to new users) — route now runs through open-weight LoRA hosting / Vertex / Bedrock; hosted-FT inference premiums can be 3-4x the base small model

fine_tune_distill.conditions: narrow stable task, eval discipline, sustained volume; break-even NOT publicly established (single-digit M tokens/month is derived arithmetic, flagged)
fine_tune_distill.quality_tradeoff: matched-or-better in-domain; brittle out-of-distribution; drift requires retraining

semantic_caching.mechanism: embed query; return stored response if similarity exceeds threshold — removes the request entirely

value.by_workload: FAQ/support 40-60%; classification 50-70%; code queries 40-60%; open-ended chat 5-15%
value.marketing_correction: vendor '95%' figures are match ACCURACY on hits, not hit FREQUENCY

semantic_caching.conditions: repetitive query distributions; threshold tuning; TTLs; personalised content excluded
semantic_caching.quality_tradeoff: REAL — false positives serve wrong/stale answers; adversarial-robustness concerns documented
semantic_caching.stacking: total = 1 - (1-hit_rate) x (1-downstream_saving); hits skew easy, cannibalising routing's cheap tier

prompt_context_compression.mechanism: small model prunes low-information tokens (LLMLingua-class) or history is truncated/summarised before the main call

value.measured: up to 20x compression at ~1.5-point benchmark loss (LLMLingua, strong on reasoning prompts, moderate on conversational); LLMLingua-2 targets 3-6x task-agnostic (unverified this sweep)

prompt_context_compression.conditions: long dynamic contexts (retrieved text, transcripts, history tails)
prompt_context_compression.quality_tradeoff: small at moderate ratios; higher when compressing instructions
prompt_context_compression.stacking: CANNIBALISES caching if applied to the stable prefix (compressed prefixes differ per request -> cache misses; and cached tokens already cost 0.1x, capping the win at <=10% of input rate). Rule: cache the stable prefix verbatim; compress only the dynamic suffix

output_control.mechanism: concise-reasoning prompting (Chain-of-Draft), style constraints, structured outputs, max_tokens backstop
output_control.applies_to: OUTPUT tokens (priced 3-6x input — proportional cuts are potent)

value.measured: Chain-of-Draft: matched CoT accuracy at as little as 7.6% of reasoning tokens (~92% cut; one measured case -92.4% tokens with accuracy UP); general concise prompting 70-90% output reduction on reasoning benchmarks
value.unmeasured: structured-outputs-vs-prose deltas — no rigorous public study (flagged)

output_control.conditions: task-dependent; naive max_tokens capping truncates mid-answer (real degradation) — it is a backstop, not a saving
output_control.quality_tradeoff: neutral-to-positive on benchmarks at moderate settings; validate per task

reasoning_effort_dial.mechanism: provider effort/thinking-budget parameter (low/medium/high) controls reasoning-token spend

value.measured: low->high spans 1.79-3.37x completion tokens on identical 225-task suite (GPT-5, Aider logs: 780K -> 2.62M tokens, $10.37 -> $29.08); medium ~ -40-50% cost vs high for -1 to -7 benchmark points; parameter ceilings allow up to ~9.5x minimal->xhigh

reasoning_effort_dial.conditions: reasoning-capable models; quality delta must be owned by the client (risk-appetite decision)

rag_retrieval_efficiency.mechanism: rerankers, contextual retrieval, chunk tuning — fewer, better chunks in context

value.measured: reranked top-k matched/exceeded quality of sending ~3x the chunks at ~1/3 context cost; contextual retrieval + rerank -67% retrieval failures (Anthropic eval)

rag_retrieval_efficiency.conditions: retrieval-based workloads; reranker adds a small per-query cost and latency
rag_retrieval_efficiency.quality_tradeoff: usually IMPROVES quality (context-rot avoidance) — the rare win-win
rag_retrieval_efficiency.stacking: complementary to caching (shrinks exactly the per-query portion caching cannot touch)

agentic_context_engineering.mechanism: context editing (clear stale tool results), deferred tool loading, programmatic tool calling, compaction, sub-agent isolation

value.measured: context editing: -84% tokens on 100-turn agentic search (vendor eval, quality UP); deferred tool loading: tool definitions 77K -> 8.7K (-85%) with accuracy up; programmatic tool calling: -37% average, to -98.7% on tool-heavy extremes
value.caveat: these CONTAIN an architecture that runs 4-15x chat token volumes — they do not make agents cheap

agentic_context_engineering.quality_tradeoff: generally positive (less context rot); compaction summaries can drop details
agentic_context_engineering.stacking: overlaps caching (rewriting history invalidates cached prefix that turn — one-off rewrite cost, net positive on long runs); the -84% and cache -90% do NOT simply add

confirmed_multiplicative: prompt_caching x batch_flex (vendor-documented, one Gemini 3.1 Pro exception)
routing/distillation rebase price; consumption levers and cache/batch percentages then apply
output_control is orthogonal to all input-side levers
same_lever_never_double: model_routing and fine_tune_distill on the same traffic
consumption levers vs the low band (the low band IS the engineered state)
cannibalisation: compression vs caching on the stable prefix (see lever entry)
semantic-cache hits skew easy and eat routing's cheap tier
compaction invalidates cache on the rewrite turn

chat_support.note: mature end accepts routing quality risk

agentic.note: contains, not cheapens — architecture runs 4-15x chat volumes

model_adjustment_factors._rule: Bands are stated for each use case's default_model + reasoning_mode. A model change is a CONSUMPTION event, not a price-sheet swap. Apply factors below; where a migration is real, re-measure (re-baseline rule).

tokenizer_cross_vendor_english.note: major tokenizers within single-digit % on English prose

tokenizer_code.note: o200k ~10-20% fewer tokens than cl100k on code; families vary

tokenizer_generation_change_same_family.note: Claude 4.6->4.7 tokenizer change at FLAT prices — an upgrade can be a silent repricing via consumption; measure on real traffic

reasoning_on_vs_off.note: reasoning tokens billed as output; R1-class thinks 12-23K tokens/question against ~0.7K visible answers

reasoning_effort_dial.note: circulating '23x between efforts' claims are ceiling-driven, not typical — treat >4x as suspect

verbosity_same_class.note: same-class models on identical tasks span 2.6x output (Artificial Analysis eval-suite token usage); GPT-5.1-style adaptive reasoning cut avg reasoning tokens ~55% vs GPT-5

agentic_turn_behaviour.note: turn count x context replay drives input; documented 5.5x cost change with output flat (Gemini 3->3.5 Flash decomposition); direction is model-specific

generation_upgrade_net.note: documented BOTH directions: -76% output at matched score (Opus 4.5 effort) vs +92% thinking tokens (R1-0528) vs >2x (Gemini 3 Flash). NEVER assume direction — re-baseline

cost_per_task_rule.note: price-per-token is NOT cost-per-task: same-score cost differences of 2-7x documented where per-token price explains under half (Aider: identical 76.9% scores at $13.75 vs $37.41; o4-mini at half o3's per-token price cost 1.4x per run). All library comparisons are $-per-transaction at stated consumption.

freshness.mechanism: hybrid — automated scan proposes, analyst disposes. Numbers change ONLY via analyst sign-off + changelog + validator pass. Operating detail: TokenEstimateLibrary_RefreshRunbook_v1.md

V1.covers: prices, cache/batch multipliers, model roster, effort-dial behaviour, provider policy (e.g. FT wind-down)

V2.covers: per-use-case token bands, hit-rate priors, optimisation savings ranges

V3.covers: conversion constants, io classes, structural rules

event_triggers: new model generation from any tracked provider (forces model_adjustment re-baseline for affected use cases)
any provider price move >=20% (precedent: GPT-5 line doubling 2026-04-23)
provider policy change touching a lever (precedent: OpenAI fine-tuning wind-down 2026-07)
new Anthropic Economic Index or equivalent first-party telemetry release

watchlist.source: Artificial Analysis model pages (eval-suite token usage, cost-to-run)
provider pricing + caching/batch docs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Bedrock)
OpenRouter rankings + third-party analyses (e.g. cache-share telemetry)
vendor per-task price pages (Intercom Fin, Zendesk, Devin, AiSDR, Harvey reporting)

freshness.client_telemetry_ratchet: every gated diagnostic that measures real client usage produces private 1A anchors -> tightens the gated library; public library receives direction-only, anonymised adjustments. The public/gated data line (concept brief §5) is unchanged.
